target.field_a: Extreme value statistics (GEV distributions, tail index analysis)
target.field_c: Proteome-wide thermal stability distributions (thermal proteome profiling)

checks.name: Data Availability — Meltome Atlas
checks.query: Can GEV be fit to real Tm distributions from the Meltome Atlas?
checks.result: Data publicly available: PRIDE PXD011929, meltomeatlas.proteomics.wzw.tum.de:5003, ProteomicsDB. Format: 48,000 proteins x 13 species, continuous Tm values (°C), ~3,700 per species. Standard GEV/GPD fitting directly applicable.

independence.evidence: TPCA detects >350 human protein complexes with correlated Tm (Mateus 2020, Lim 2023). 60-80% of human proteins in complexes. Complex subunits co-aggregate.
independence.mitigation: Cluster maxima method (min Tm per complex); extremal index estimation (Ferro & Segers 2003). Generator should use complex-level EVT.

stationarity.evidence: Protein Tm is fixed intrinsic property. Per-species analysis has no temporal trend.

domain_of_attraction.evidence: Tm bounded (~0-120C). Expect Weibull domain (xi < 0). FTG theorem applies universally.

censoring.evidence: ~20% proteins outside 30-90C measurement window. ~10% left-censored (Tm<30C) = most vulnerable proteins. Exactly the tail EVT targets.
censoring.mitigation: Censored likelihood GEV (Smith 1985); POT with observable lower tail; censoring proportion itself is a return-level prediction

queries.terms: "GEV distribution" OR "Gumbel distribution" OR "extreme value statistics" AND proteome AND thermal

queries.note: BLAST applies EVT to sequence alignment scores, NOT protein melting temperatures. Does not bridge the fields.

checks.name: STRING/TPCA — Independence Assumption (Complex Co-aggregation)
checks.method: TPCA literature review (STRING API unavailable; used published TPCA results)
checks.result: TPCA (Mateus 2020, Lim 2023) confirms >350 human protein complexes with correlated melting curves. Proteasome, ribosome, spliceosome, ATP synthase all verified. Intra-complex Tm spread reflects functional subunit architecture.
checks.quantitative_impact: Extremal index theta ~0.5-0.7 estimated for human proteome. Effective sample size reduced by 1.5-2x.
checks.biological_insight: Complex-minimum Tm (thermal bottleneck subunit) is more meaningful EVT variable than individual protein Tm. Enables pathway-level thermal vulnerability mapping.
checks.verdict: PARTIAL_VIOLATION — not a blocker; complex-level EVT is a richer analysis

checks.name: Sample Size Sufficiency for GEV Estimation

key_concerns: Left-censoring: ~10% proteins with Tm<30C are exactly the vulnerable tail EVT targets. Requires censored likelihood GEV methods (addressable, standard in hydrology).
Independence: protein complex co-aggregation (~350+ complexes). Use cluster-maxima (min Tm per complex) to restore approximate independence AND gain biological interpretability.
generator_recommendations: Apply GEV/GPD to complex-minimum Tm (thermal bottleneck) rather than raw protein-level Tm.
Use censored GEV estimation for the 10% left-censored proteins — frame as methodological novelty.
Generate prediction: thermophile proteomes have more negative xi (lighter left tail) than mesophile proteomes.
Generate prediction: complex-minimum Tm return levels predict temperature at which specific cellular processes (translation, proteasome, OXPHOS) become compromised.
Generate prediction: POT exceedances (anomalously low-Tm proteins) enriched for intrinsically disordered regions and regulatory proteins.
Do NOT include BLAST Gumbel as prior work — it applies EVT to alignment scores, not thermal stability.
